findings.title: Pipeline Consolidation Summary
findings.description: Consolidated 41 findings from 7 agents into 35 unique findings. Removed 6 duplicates. Identified 4 cross-agent conflicts.

findings.title: Top Priority Findings
findings.description: Highest priority items based on weighted scoring (user_impact×0.35 + business_value×0.30) / (effort×0.20 + risk×0.15): ClearFeed's Privacy-First Approach is Gaining Traction (score=3.792); Primary KPI: Content Click-Through Rate (CTR) (score=3.593); High Demand for Personalization (score=3.593); North Star Metric: User Engagement Score (score=3.154); Phased Delivery Plan (score=3.129)

top_priorities.title: ClearFeed's Privacy-First Approach is Gaining Traction
North Star Metric: User Engagement Score

findings.title: Cross-Agent Conflict Resolution Report
findings.description: Resolved 4 conflicts between agent findings. requirements-006 vs feasibility-001: The requirement for a GDPR compliance audit is critical for legal compliance, but it is also identified as a blocker for the EU market launch.; requirements-001 vs risk-004: The user behavior tracking pipeline requires extensive data collection, while there are significant privacy risks associated with user tracking.; customer-003 vs risk-001: The need for clear consent mechanisms is highlighted, yet the current consent mechanism is inadequate and poses legal compliance risks.

evidence.excerpt: The requirement for a GDPR compliance audit is critical for legal compliance, but it is also identified as a blocker for the EU market launch.
The user behavior tracking pipeline requires extensive data collection, while there are significant privacy risks associated with user tracking.
The need for clear consent mechanisms is highlighted, yet the current consent mechanism is inadequate and poses legal compliance risks.
The lack of a tiered consent mechanism is a gap, while a GDPR compliance audit is required to address legal concerns.

conflicts.description: The requirement for a GDPR compliance audit is critical for legal compliance, but it is also identified as a blocker for the EU market launch.
conflicts.resolution: Conduct the GDPR compliance audit as a priority before any further development or market launch activities.
conflicts.reasoning: Legal compliance is paramount and must be addressed before proceeding with any features that rely on user data.

conflicts.description: The user behavior tracking pipeline requires extensive data collection, while there are significant privacy risks associated with user tracking.
conflicts.resolution: Implement strong data anonymization techniques and user consent mechanisms to mitigate privacy risks while developing the tracking pipeline.
conflicts.reasoning: Balancing the need for user behavior data with privacy concerns is essential to maintain user trust and comply with regulations.

conflicts.description: The need for clear consent mechanisms is highlighted, yet the current consent mechanism is inadequate and poses legal compliance risks.
conflicts.resolution: Revamp the consent mechanism to ensure it meets GDPR requirements for granularity and provides users with clear options.
conflicts.reasoning: Addressing the inadequacy of the consent mechanism is crucial for user trust and legal compliance.

conflicts.description: The lack of a tiered consent mechanism is a gap, while a GDPR compliance audit is required to address legal concerns.
conflicts.resolution: Develop a tiered consent mechanism as part of the GDPR compliance audit process to ensure legal adherence and user control.
conflicts.reasoning: Integrating the development of a tiered consent mechanism within the compliance audit will streamline efforts to meet regulatory requirements.

summary: Consolidated 41 findings into 35 unique items. Removed 6 duplicates, resolved 4 conflicts. Top priority: ClearFeed's Privacy-First Approach is Gaining Traction.
